title: The cartouche is the oldest known symbol of sealed sovereign authority — in ancient practice, a cartouche enclosed a ruling name: to be within it was to be under that jurisdiction. Here, the oval is Precept's definition: the sealed governing boundary around the domain. The bold horizontal rule inside is the precept itself — the one governing line that holds within the contract and cannot be overridden from outside.
conceptualBridge: The cartouche means Precept's definition is a sealed governing authority because the oval enclosure is the jurisdictional boundary and the horizontal rule inside is the precept that holds structurally within it.
decision: Kept: heraldic oval-seal family is new to the set, conceptual bridge is historically grounded and specific; at threshold (4.0) — worth refining the distinct silhouette before promoting.
